demo.tooltip: Demo mode is active. Viewing, search, and dashboards work normally. Creating, editing, deleting, running scans, training detectors, and the browser assistant are disabled.
demo.mcpNotice: Demo mode is active. MCP read-only access works with an existing token, but creating, editing, or deleting tokens is disabled.
demo.blockedDescription: This operation is not available in demo mode. Create, update, and delete operations are disabled.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Instance-wide defaults, AI providers, and connected MCP servers.

version.heading: Software Version
version.desc: The currently installed version of Classifyre running on this instance.
version.updateDesc: A newer version of Classifyre is available.

settings.regionDesc: Language, clock, and timezone used across the instance.

assistant.subtitle: AI Assistant
assistant.title: Assistant model
assistant.description: Control the in-app assistant and the model it runs on.
assistant.enableDesc: When off, assistant features are hidden across the app.
assistant.modelDesc: The assistant uses this provider for completions.
assistant.noProviders: No AI providers yet. Create one to power the assistant.
assistant.disabledHint: Enable the assistant to choose a model.

autopilot.subtitle: Investigation Autopilot
autopilot.title: Autopilot
autopilot.description: Autonomous AI agents that manage inquiries and investigation cases after every scan. All actions are attributed to the AI and fully audited.
autopilot.inquiryEnableDesc: After each scan, the AI reviews new findings, creates or enriches inquiries, avoids duplicates and flags when a case is warranted.
autopilot.inquiryDesiredPlaceholder: Describe what the AI should focus on investigating, e.g. leaked credentials in customer-facing spaces, PII in public docs…
autopilot.inquirySearchablePlaceholder: Describe what data exists and is worth matching, e.g. Confluence spaces with API docs, Jira projects with support tickets…
autopilot.caseEnableDesc: The AI builds and advances investigation cases from inquiries: hypotheses, evidence, findings, notes, edges and status changes.
autopilot.caseGuidancePlaceholder: Extra instructions for case management, e.g. severity conventions, when to close a case, naming rules…
autopilot.requiresAi: Enable the AI assistant and select a provider to use the autopilot.
autopilot.aiModeDesc: Inherit follows the instance setting. Observe only lets the AI read but never change this item.

sources.title: Sources
sources.description: Manage your data sources and connections

form.nameHelp: Give your source a memorable name to identify it
form.descriptionHelp: Optional. Add notes about what this source is for.

sources.noSourcesHint: Adjust the filters or add a new source.
sources.deleted: Source deleted. Related assets and findings were removed.

delete.title: Delete source?
delete.permanentBody: Deleting this source will permanently remove the source, all related assets, and all findings.

columns.lastRunStatsDesc: Assets created / updated · Findings from latest run

detail.requiredDetailsDesc: Core required configuration for this source.
detail.notScannedMessage: This source has not been scanned yet.

edit.updateSettings: Update connection settings for this source
edit.detectorConfigDesc: Choose which detectors to run and fine-tune their thresholds

new.title: Add New Source
new.description: Connect a new data source to start scanning for issues
new.incompleteSettings: Source settings are incomplete. Please fix validation errors and try again.

testConnection.descTesting: Checking your source settings and verifying connectivity.
testConnection.descOk: The source is reachable and ready to use.
testConnection.descError: The source test failed. Review the returned error details below.

stepper.sourceDetailsDesc: Required, secrets, and optional source settings.

scanConfig.browseDesc: Search across built-in and custom detectors, then tune each one without switching tabs.
scanConfig.presetsDesc: Pick a business-ready baseline, then customize only where needed.
scanConfig.advancedSettingsDesc: Thresholds, model parameters, and pattern-level controls.
scanConfig.noDetectors: No detectors match this search in {{group}}.
scanConfig.noDetectorsHint: Try a broader search term or switch to another category.
scanConfig.customDetectorsDesc: Reusable detectors from the shared catalog. Select them per source and manage training separately.
scanConfig.loadError: Custom detectors could not be loaded.
scanConfig.noCustom: No custom detectors match this search.
scanConfig.noCustomHint: Try a broader search term or create a new detector in the catalog.
scanConfig.noSelectableCustomHint: No active custom detectors are available for this source. Create a new one or manage the catalog.
scanConfig.noSchemasHint: Add detector schemas to configure scan coverage.
scanConfig.fallbackDesc: Reusable detector from the shared custom detector catalog.
scanConfig.searchPlaceholder: Search detectors, presets, categories, or custom detector keys...

sampling.title: Sampling
sampling.enableOcrDesc: Extract text from images and scanned PDFs using optical character recognition (slower, requires OCR dependencies).
sampling.enableTranscriptionDesc: Transcribe audio and video files to text before detection (slower, requires transcription dependencies).
sampling.orderByDesc: Column for date ordering (auto-detected if blank)
sampling.rowsPerPageDesc: Databases: sample size (RANDOM/LATEST) or batch size (ALL). Files: rows/lines per detector page.
sampling.hintRandom2: Picks a random subset of items each run
sampling.hintLatest2: Fetches the most recently modified items first
sampling.hintAll2: Scans every item — use with care on large sources

scanWizard.title: Create New Scan
scanWizard.description: Choose a source and run a real scan
scanWizard.instruction: Select one source to run. Running sources are locked.
scanWizard.noSourcesHint: Add a source first before creating a scan.

findings.title: Findings
findings.description: Unified findings table with server-side filtering, plus overview charts and stats.
findings.severityTimeline: Severity timeline for the selected window.
findings.noTrendData: No trend data available for the selected scope.
findings.noFindingsHint: Adjust the filters and run the search again.
findings.selectAll: Select all findings matching current filter

bulkUpdate.title: Bulk Update Findings
bulkUpdate.onlyFilledFields: . Only filled fields will be updated.
bulkUpdate.willApply: Changes will be applied to all {{count}} findings.
bulkUpdate.selectAtLeastOne: Select at least one field to update.
bulkUpdate.commentPlaceholder: Add a comment to all selected findings…

extraction.noFieldsHint: The extractor ran but returned no field values for this finding.

detail.coreMetadata: Core metadata, location, and detection signals.
detail.historyDesc: Every status change and detection touchpoint.
detail.noHistoryHint: History tracking hasn't been recorded for this finding yet.
detail.tryAgain: Check the finding and try again.

drawer.title: Details

drawer.commentPlaceholder: Add a note to this finding…

signals.desc: Structured metadata captured by the detector.

assets.noVisualizationHint: Assets table is still available below for direct lookup.
assets.noAssetsHint: Adjust the filters and run the search again.

columns.sourceTypeDesc: The type of data source this asset belongs to
columns.detectorsDesc: Active detectors that fired on this asset and their finding counts
columns.topFindingsDesc: Most frequent finding types on this asset
columns.severityDesc: Highest severity finding and total findings count
columns.statusMixDesc: Breakdown of finding statuses on this asset

subTable.noFindings: No findings returned for this asset

graph.noDataHint: Assets are available but there are no links to visualize.

detail.title: Asset Details
detail.metadataDesc: Core metadata and source context for this asset.

detectors.title: Custom Detectors

visual.tooltip: Processes images visually rather than as text

detectors.search: Search detector name, key, description, sources
detectors.noDetectorsHint: Try changing search text or filter values.
detectors.selectTypeDesc: Select the detection method that best fits your use case. You can change the configuration later.

methods.rulesetsDesc: Regex and keyword logic for deterministic pattern matching.
methods.rulesetsLong: Define keywords and regular expressions to detect specific text patterns. Fully deterministic — no model required. Ideal for compliance patterns, PII, secrets, or any predictable structure.
methods.classifiersDesc: Label-based text classification with training examples.
methods.classifiersLong: Train a machine learning model to categorize text into your own labels. Supports zero-shot classification out of the box and fine-tuning with your own examples. Best for intent detection, topic routing, or tone analysis.
methods.entityDesc: Named entity extraction with custom label vocabularies.
methods.entityLong: Extract named entities from text using a pre-trained GLiNER model with your custom label set. Great for finding people, organizations, products, codes, or any concept you define.

editor.methodSetupDesc: Configure method-specific logic and detector identity.
editor.patternSeverityDesc: Tune severity, confidence, and language coverage.
editor.searchExamples: Search examples by use-case or method
editor.configJsonDesc: Raw detector config. Switch back to Builder to parse and sync it.

tests.inputPlaceholder: Paste text to test against the detector...

extraction.noDataHint: Extractions are generated during source scans once an extractor is configured.

detectors.description: Reusable trainable detectors shared across sources. Click any row to open detector details.
detectors.addNewDesc: Create reusable custom detection logic with rulesets, classifiers, or entity extraction.
detectors.deleteImpact: Deleting this detector will remove it from all these sources. Those sources will no longer detect this pattern.

regex.identityDescription: Give your detector a name, stable key, and optional description.
regex.name: Name
regex.descriptionPlaceholder: Detect leaked API keys and tokens in documents
regex.patternsHint: Define named regex patterns. Each pattern is matched independently and produces findings with the configured severity. Uses RE2 syntax in production.
regex.patternDescriptionPlaceholder: Human-readable description of what this pattern matches
regex.testDescription: Try your patterns against sample text
regex.testHint: Paste sample text below to test your patterns. Matches are highlighted in real-time.
regex.re2Warning: This preview uses browser-native regex. Production uses RE2 — behavior may differ for capture groups and dot-newline matching.
regex.sampleTextPlaceholder: Paste or type sample text to test your patterns against...
regex.noMatches: No matches found in the sample text.
regex.validationPatternRequired: At least one pattern is required

transformer.name: Name
transformer.keyHint: Auto-derived from name if left blank
transformer.severityMapHint: Map predicted labels to severity levels. First matching rule wins. Unmatched labels receive info by default.
transformer.validationModelRequired: Model ID is required for this pipeline type

llm.name: Name
llm.keyHint: Auto-derived from name if left blank
llm.providerHint: Manage credentials in Settings → AI Provider.
llm.providerVisionHint: This provider accepts image/PDF input. Image and PDF assets are rendered to images and sent to the model directly instead of using text extraction.
llm.systemPromptPlaceholder: Describe what the model should classify and extract.
llm.labelsHint: The labels the model may assign. Each predicted label becomes a finding.
llm.severityMapHint: Map predicted labels to severity levels. First matching rule wins; unmatched labels use the default severity.
llm.outputHint: Structured properties the model extracts. Stored in finding metadata and extracted data.

detectors.startBlankDesc: Configure the detector from scratch with no pre-filled values.
detectors.chooseTemplateDesc: Pick a pre-built example to start from, or configure from scratch.

gliner2.title: GLiNER2 Pipeline
gliner2.description: Define entities to extract and classification tasks — all run in a single model pass. Ideal for structured information extraction from unstructured text, with no training data required.

regex.title: Regex Patterns
regex.description: Define precise pattern-matching rules using regular expressions. Fast, deterministic, zero ML overhead. Perfect for codes, IDs, and structured formats like IBANs or order numbers.

llm.title: AI Detector
llm.description: Use a large language model with a natural-language prompt to classify content and extract structured fields. Best for nuanced, context-dependent detection where examples and rules are hard to define explicitly.

text_classification.title: Text Classification
text_classification.description: Run any HuggingFace text-classification model. Map predicted labels to severity levels. Ideal for spam detection, toxicity, sentiment, and custom topic classifiers.

image_classification.title: Image Classification
image_classification.tagline: Label images using a vision model
image_classification.description: Classify images with any HuggingFace vision model. Useful for NSFW detection, harmful content filtering, and custom image category labelling.

feature_extraction.title: Feature Extraction
feature_extraction.description: Embed text into dense vectors using any HuggingFace sentence-transformer. Store embeddings as findings for downstream semantic search or clustering.

object_detection.title: Object Detection
object_detection.tagline: Locate and label objects in images
object_detection.description: Run any HuggingFace object-detection model on images. Findings include bounding boxes, confidence scores, and label-based severity mapping.
object_detection.tags: Bounding boxes, Object labels, Severity map

scans.title: Scans
scans.subtitle: Search run history and filter by operational status from one surface.
scans.runTrendDescription: Status distribution over the selected timeline.

runnerAssets.searchPlaceholder: Search by asset name or hash…
runnerAssets.noAssets: No assets processed in this run
runnerAssets.noAssetsHint: Adjust the filters or wait for processing to begin.

scans.noRunsHint: Use filters below to find runs, or start a new scan.
scans.sourceUnavailable: Source is not available for this run

sandbox.dropFiles: Drop files here or click to select

runs.noRunsHint: Adjust filters to broaden the run search.
runs.rescanStarted: Re-scan started — findings will be appended

sandbox.monitorDesc: Monitor sandbox runs and inspect detector findings
sandbox.autoRefresh: Runs auto-refresh while processing. Expand completed rows to inspect findings.

notifications.description: Operational feed for scan events, findings, source changes, and system signals.

notifications.searchNote: Search is applied to the current page

runners.noRunsHint: Adjust filters to broaden the run search.

logs.title: Logs
logs.description: Parsed runner logs with level extraction
logs.noLogsHint: No log entries match your search.
logs.noRowsToCopy: Select at least one row to copy
logs.noStorageWarningBody: This instance has no object storage (S3) configured. Scan logs are visible in real time during a run, but are not retained once the run completes — refreshing this page will show an empty log. To enable persistent log storage, configure an S3-compatible provider for your deployment.

cases.noInvestigationsHint: Adjust filters to broaden your search.

evidence.noEvidenceHint: Pull from a linked inquiry or add assets manually.

tooltips.entity: Asset or entity used as evidence
tooltips.findings: Attached findings · chip shows addable count
tooltips.note: Analyst note, click pencil to edit

tooltips.detectorType: Detector type that fired on this finding
tooltips.matchedContent: Content snippet that triggered this finding
tooltips.severity: Risk level assigned to this finding
tooltips.note: Analyst note, click pencil to edit
tooltips.added: When this finding was attached to the case

forms.sourceNameDesc: Give this source a clear, unique name so it stands out later.
forms.additionalConfigDesc: General optional fields that fine-tune this source.
forms.namePlaceholder: Give your source a memorable name to identify it
forms.optionalParametersDesc: Additional settings you can configure when the default connection setup is not enough.
forms.validationFailed: Validation failed. Complete all required fields before testing or saving.

ai.ask: Ask a question about this section...
ai.assistantQuickStartDesc: Pick a template or start from blank. You can override everything later.
ai.startBlankDescription: Begin with an empty form and configure the source from scratch.
ai.noTemplatesAvailable: No templates available for this source yet.

mcp.tokenCreated: MCP token created. Copy it now because it will not be shown again.
mcp.title: MCP Server
mcp.exposeDescLong: Create one-time bearer tokens for IDEs, agents, and workspace-specific MCP clients. Tokens are generated by the API, stored only as hashes, and the plaintext value is returned once at creation time.
mcp.disabledNotice: MCP is disabled. The /mcp endpoint returns 503 until you re-enable it above.
mcp.tokenReady: {{name}} is ready. This token is shown only once.
mcp.tokenReadyDesc: Copy it into your MCP client or secret manager now. After you dismiss this message, only the masked preview remains.
mcp.noTokens: No MCP tokens yet. Create one per client or workspace so you can rotate and revoke access cleanly.
mcp.createDesc: The API generates the raw token, returns it once for copying, and stores only a hash plus masked preview.
mcp.bestPracticeTip: Best practice: issue one token per MCP client, workspace, or environment so you can rotate and revoke them independently.
mcp.editDesc: Update the display name or access state. Raw token material is not stored and cannot be recovered.
mcp.allowAuthDesc: Disable this token to revoke access without deleting the record.
mcp.deleteDesc: Delete {{name}} and permanently invalidate its bearer token.
mcp.deleteDescGeneral: Delete this token and permanently invalidate its bearer token.
mcp.deleteWarning: Deleted tokens cannot be recovered. Any MCP client using this token will stop authenticating immediately.

aiProvider.title: AI Provider
aiProvider.configurationDesc: Configure which AI provider and model the API uses for assistant features. The API key is stored encrypted and never returned in plaintext.
aiProvider.failedToLoad: Failed to load AI provider settings
aiProvider.baseUrlDesc: Endpoint used for OpenAI-compatible requests (e.g. OpenRouter, Ollama, Azure OpenAI).
aiProvider.keyStored: Key stored. Click the field to replace it.
aiProvider.keyNotStored: The key is encrypted at rest and never returned.
aiProvider.needApiKey: Enter or keep an API key to test the provider
aiProvider.testDescription: Send a test code task to the configured AI provider
aiProvider.sectionDesc: Manage reusable AI provider credentials. Pick one as the default used by the assistant and other AI features. Keys are stored encrypted and never returned in plaintext.
aiProvider.name: Name
aiProvider.supportsVisionDesc: Enable when this model accepts images. Detectors using this credential send rendered file images (PDF pages, images) to the model instead of extracted text.
aiProvider.noProviders: No AI providers yet. Add one to get started.
aiProvider.defaultDesc: Used by the AI assistant and other AI features.
aiProvider.deleteConfirm: This permanently removes "{{name}}". Any feature using it as the default will fall back to none.
aiProvider.manageDesc: Reusable credentials. Keys are encrypted at rest and never returned.
aiProvider.deleteInUseHint: Reassign the detectors using this provider before deleting it.
aiProvider.deleteAssistantHint: This is the assistant model — pick another first.

csvExport.label: Download CSV
csvExport.title: Export to CSV
csvExport.description: {{count}} {{entityLabel}} matching the current filters will be exported. Large exports stream directly to your download. You can also connect live with Excel to pull this data — see the

discovery.title: Overview
discovery.firstScan: Run your first scan to surface high-risk assets.

page.description: Start with an inquiry — a saved question over your findings. When the matches warrant a deeper look, open a case to collect evidence, weigh hypotheses, and reach a conclusion.

caseDetail.emptyGraphDesc: Add evidence to seed the relationship graph — pull matches from a linked inquiry or attach findings directly.
caseDetail.conclusionDesc: Summarize what the evidence shows, which hypothesis it supports, and how strongly. Closing the case archives its inquiry.
caseDetail.closeCaseDesc: The conclusion is recorded, the case is marked closed and its linked inquiry is archived (it stops surfacing new matches). You can reopen later.
caseDetail.conclusionRequired: a conclusion is required to close
caseDetail.noInquiryLinked: No inquiry linked — evidence is added manually. Link one below to pull its matches as evidence.
caseDetail.unlinkInquiryDesc: The inquiry and the evidence already pulled from it are kept — only the link to this case is removed.
caseDetail.evidenceTabDesc: The persisted record of this case — snapshots survive even if the source data changes. Pull from the driving inquiry on the Overview tab, or attach findings directly.
caseDetail.newMatchesBanner: {{count}} new match appeared in the linked inquiries since last review.

addEvidence.title: Add evidence
addEvidence.attachFindingsDesc: Findings already in the case are hidden. The matching asset is added as evidence automatically.

inquiryDetail.deleteDesc: The saved query and its match history are removed. Evidence already pulled into cases is kept.
inquiryDetail.matchesLiveDesc: Matches are computed live — they are not evidence yet. Open a case to start an investigation and keep a snapshot of what you select.
inquiryDetail.alreadyInCase: Rows marked "in case" are already evidence in {{title}}.

newCase.title: Open a case
newCase.description: A case is the workspace for one investigation: it keeps its own copy of evidence, tracks hypotheses and discussion, and ends with a conclusion. Link any number of inquiries to drive it — or start blank and add evidence directly.
newCase.descriptionPlaceholder: Context, scope, what triggered this investigation…
newCase.inquiryDrivesMany: An inquiry can drive several cases at once.
newCase.emptyStartDesc: This case starts empty. Select inquiries on the left to bring their matches in as initial evidence — or add evidence directly from the case workspace later.
newCase.selectionDesc: Selected matches are copied into the case as evidence — the case keeps its own snapshot, and you can pull newer matches at any time.
newCase.caseOpenedWithFindings: Case opened — {{count}} finding(s) added as evidence
newCase.pullFailed: Case opened, but some evidence could not be pulled — pull again from the case.

toolbar.connectTitle: Click a source node, then a target node, to create an edge
toolbar.pathTitle: Click two nodes to highlight the path between them (or shift-click in select mode)
toolbar.collapseFindings: Collapse all findings into their assets

edgeDetail.inferredReadOnly: Inferred edges are derived from the data and cannot be edited. Create a manual edge to record your own relationship.

contextMenu.inferredReadOnly: Inferred edges are auto-generated and read-only. Create a manual edge instead.

graphView.findingNotResolved: Finding was attached but could not be resolved
graphView.noPath: No connection between these nodes in the current view

sidebar.noHypotheses: None yet. Hypotheses let you group evidence into competing explanations.
sidebar.legendUnattached: findings not yet attached — click to review

attachDialog.title: Unattached findings
attachDialog.description: {{label}} has {{count}} finding(s) not in this case yet. Attach the relevant ones as evidence.

newHypothesisDialog.title: New hypothesis
newHypothesisDialog.linkedNode: "{{label}}" will be linked to it as the first piece of evidence.
newHypothesisDialog.description: A competing explanation you can attach supporting or contradicting evidence to.
newHypothesisDialog.titlePlaceholder: e.g. Credentials leaked through CI logs
newHypothesisDialog.statementPlaceholder: What exactly does this hypothesis claim, and what would prove or refute it?

linkHypothesisDialog.title: Link to hypothesis
linkHypothesisDialog.recordDesc: Record what "{{label}}" means for a hypothesis.
linkHypothesisDialog.addedIfNeeded: It will be added as evidence if it isn't yet.
linkHypothesisDialog.notePlaceholder: Why does this evidence matter for the hypothesis?
